question: Does the image feature a horizontal canvas with a white background, divided into a main map on the left (approx. 70%) and a column of three vertically stacked panels on the right (approx. 30%)?
Are the three auxiliary panels on the right framed in thin gray lines and properly aligned vertically?
Does the main map depict a topographic contour map with brown-gray thin strokes, featuring one hill (concentric contours) and one U-shaped valley?
Are index contours on the main map numerically labeled with values indicating an interval of 20 (e.g., 100, 120, 140, 160, 180)?
Does a blue river wind through the valley bottom on the main map and exit the boundary?
Is there a black road crossing the river on the main map, with the bridge specifically indicated by two short parallel lines?
Is a coordinate grid overlaid on the main map with columns labeled A-D and rows labeled 1-4 on the border frame?
Is there a North arrow labeled 'N' in the upper right corner and a scale bar labeled '0-1-2 km' in the lower left corner of the main map?
Is there a thick black cross-section line labeled 'A' and 'B' at its ends drawn on the main map?
Does the top-right panel (Location Inset) show a simplified rectangular outline with a highlighted box indicating the main map extent and a pointing arrow?
Does the middle-right panel (Terrain Profile) display axes labeled 'DIST' (horizontal) and 'ELEV' (vertical)?
Does the bottom-right panel (Climate Chart) have a horizontal axis labeled with months 1-12?
Does the Climate Chart feature a dual-axis layout with 'TEMP' (left vertical) for a line graph and 'PREC' (right vertical) for exactly 12 bar columns?
Are the colors strictly applied as: terrain lines brown-gray, river blue, and all other elements black/gray?
Is the image completely free of shadows and gradients, adhering to a flat vector line art style?
Does all text in the image consist exclusively of Latin letters and numerals?
